title: $rscan
text: $rscan Find a substring within a string, starting from the end of the string. $rscan ( Source , SearchFor ) Example: vOutput = $rscan ("The quick brown fox", "quick") Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description Source String String in which the substring needs to be found. SearchFor String Substring to search for. SearchFor can be a constant string, regex string, or syntax string. Return Values Position of the substring within the string. Use Allowed in all component types. Description The $rscan function looks for a string or a pattern in a string starting from the end of the string. Example: Using $rscan The following example finds the first occurrence of literal o in the source string, when scanning from right to left. vOutput = $rscan("look for o", "o") ; vOutput = 10 Copy The following example finds the literal string quick in the source string. OUTPUT = $rscan ("The quick brown fox", "quick") ; OUTPUT = 5 Copy The following example finds a number (using the syntax string "#" ) in the source string. It returns the position of first number it encounters when scanning from right to left. vOutput = $rscan ("The 1st quick brown fox came 2nd.", $syntax("#")) ; OUTPUT = 30 Copy The following example finds the first occurrence of regex o in the source string, when scanning from right to left. Explain this code vOutput = $rscan("look for o",$regex("o")) ; vOutput = 10 Copy The following example finds the first occurrence of regex o in the source string with case-insensitive, when scanning from right to left. Explain this code vOutput = $rscan("look for O",$regex("o","i")) ; vOutput = 10 Copy Related Links $scan Returns the position of a substring within a string. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
